data.title: XRP Analyst Says ‘We Will All Be Surprised’ By October With This Breakout
data.description:
Dark Defender, a prominent XRP analyst, has drawn significant attention to the token, suggesting that XRP may be setting up a move that could take the market by surprise. Despite its struggle to decisively break above the $3 mark, XRP is now forming a Falling Wedge pattern that signals the potential for a powerful breakout by October. 

Falling Wedge Signals XRP Breakout By October 

In a recent XRP price analysis, published on Monday, Dark Defender noted that the third-largest cryptocurrency has once again respected its key support levels at $2.85, despite being rejected at $3.13. The XRP price tapped into the primary support trendline, highlighted in orange on the chart, which has consistently held as a structural base.

Importantly, the Relative Strength Index (RSI) is edging closer to oversold territory, a signal often associated with potential price reversals. While skepticism spreads across the market, Dark Defender insists that the XRP bullish structure remains intact and that disbelief itself is a sign that many could be caught off guard by what’s to come. 

The analyst notes that the cryptocurrency has been consolidating within a Falling Wedge pattern between July and September. According to him, October could be the month when XRP finally breaks free from the wedge and delivers a rally strong enough to surprise the broader market. 

Building on this momentum, Dark Defender has forecasted three potential price targets for XRP by October: $4.17, $4.92, and $5.85. These levels correspond to upper Fibonacci Extension zones, specifically 261.8%, 361.8%, and 236.8%, respectively. Meanwhile, XRP has its closest supports set at $2.80 and $2.64, ensuring a strong base for the projected Falling Wedge breakout. 

XRP Bullish Run Just Starting

Following his earlier predictions, Dark Defender further reinforced his bullish case by asserting that XRP has not begun its true rally. He suggested a power shift is underway, hinting that what the market has seen so far is only the beginning of a larger bullish wave. 

This perspective arrives at a time when XRP has already delivered a remarkable performance in 2025. According to CoinMarketCap’s data, the cryptocurrency has gained 384% Year-to-Date, a surge fueled by increasing demand, rumors of a potential XRP ETF, and renewed confidence after the conclusion of the Ripple-SEC lawsuit. 

With XRP reaching a high of $3.65 earlier this year and trading just over 5% shy of reclaiming its former all-time high of $3.84, Dark Defender remains certain that the asset’s real growth is still ahead. Based on this view, the recent pullback below $3 is not seen as a weakness, but rather as a consolidation phase before the next leg higher. 

He highlighted that XRP is nearing the end of this corrective ABCDE consolidation phase and preparing for a lift-off. According to his chart analysis, once the cryptocurrency reclaims the $3.33 level, it could pave the way for double-digit prices.

data.title: Crypto Market Prediction: Shiba Inu (SHIB) $0.00001 Bottom, Ethereum (ETH) Loses $4,000, Bitcoin (BTC): Head and Shoulders to $123,000?
data.description:
The market is expiriencing somewhat of a storm as Shiba Inu, Ethereum and Bitcoin are losing multiple key support levels, and there is a good possibility of an aggravation here as no fresh inflows are present and most of the volume on the market is on the selling side. 

Shiba Inu loses key support

The price of Shiba Inu has fallen below important support levels, indicating that a retest of the $0.00001 bottom may be closer than many anticipated. This indicates that the stock is once again under strong selling pressure. According to the asset's current structure, if sentiment and technicals do not rapidly improve, the asset may be headed for new 2025 lows. The symmetrical triangle structure that had previously kept the price of SHIB stable for months has now been broken on the daily chart.  Chart by TradingView">

The token broke because it was unable to hold above the 50-day and 100-day EMAs, making it susceptible to additional drops. There is still momentum working against bulls because the 200-day EMA is likewise sloping downward. Previously a dependable short-term floor, the $0.0000122 support zone is now resistance as bears gain ground. Red candles have seen an increase in volume, suggesting that sellers are growing more confident.

Although the RSI has entered oversold territory, it has not yet indicated a reversal, suggesting that the downward momentum may continue. SHIB may be headed for a test of $0.0000115 if the current circumstances continue with the possibility of a decline to the psychological $0.00001 level. In addition to representing a retest of SHIB's annual lows, such a decline might put the asset in danger of breaching its larger 2025 support range. Recovery appears to be difficult for now.

Ethereum stumbles

The fact that Ethereum has dropped below the crucial $4,000 mark suggests that the market as a whole is weak and that more declines are likely. The decline occurred quickly after ETH failed to maintain its consolidation around the $4,400-$4,500 resistance zone, and bearish pressure took over.

Since its recent symmetrical triangle formation, ETH has been declining sharply, according to the daily chart. With sellers taking charge, this breakdown demonstrates that there is no buying support at higher levels. The bearish move has gained more weight as trading volumes have increased during the decline. Chart by TradingView">

The Relative Strength Index, meanwhile, has dipped nearer to oversold territory, indicating that bearish momentum may yet worsen before a relief bounce takes place. Ethereum is in a precarious position right now, trading just below $4,000.

The next significant area of interest, if selling persists, is around the 100-day EMA, which is close to $3,833. This moving average has historically served as a dependable level of support during periods of correction, so buyers may intervene there to protect against further losses. Ethereum might level off and try to push back toward $4,200 if the 100 EMA holds.

It is impossible to rule out a more aggressive move toward the $3,600-$3,400 range if this support fails. The 200 EMA would then be the crucial last line of defense to prevent a protracted bearish cycle further below it, at $3,392.

For the time being, Ethereum’s failure to hold the $4,000 mark is a serious setback to bullish sentiment. Investors should closely monitor ETH’s response to the $3,833 mark in the upcoming sessions. Hopes for a midterm recovery could be raised by a strong bounce here, but failure would pave the way for a more significant correction.

Bitcoin pattern recognized

A head and shoulders pattern could determine whether the next move is a surge toward $123,000 or a plunge into bearish territory, which may be its most important formation of the year.

On the daily chart, the pattern has been gradually developing, with Bitcoin settling between $112,000 and $114,000 following several unsuccessful attempts to rise. Bitcoin is currently trading just above the 100 EMA, and the pattern’s neckline is a crucial support level.

The bullish head and shoulders scenario could be confirmed by a clear breakout above the $114,000 resistance, which would pave the way for a medium-term move to $123,000. This level is the next logical target for bulls, since it is where breakout traders and upside liquidity are likely to converge.

But prudence is still necessary. The danger of a decline will increase rapidly if the pattern does not finish and Bitcoin drops below the neckline. The next important level of support is the 200 EMA, which is presently trading at about $106,000. A decline to that level would push the market into a bearish narrative and put investor confidence to the test, even though this would still keep Bitcoin above its longer-term bullish structure.

Hesitance is also suggested by volume trends: selling spikes imply that whales are offloading at every rally attempt, and buying pressure has not been strong enough to break through resistance levels.

data.title: JAPAN SEPT TOKYO CPI: ENERGY PRICE RISE IN PAYBACK FOR SEPT 2024 NATIONWIDE SUBSIDIES FOR ELECTRICITY, NATURAL GAS PUSHING DOWN ENERGY BY 5.3%
Costco's same-store sales miss again, but it sees benefits from extra hours, Instacart deals
data.description:
By Bill Peters

Ahead of its quarterly earnings, the membership warehouse chain faced concerns about slower growth

Costco's e-commerce sales were up 13.6% during the fourth quarter.

Costco Wholesale Corp. on Thursday reported fiscal fourth-quarter sales that came up shy of Wall Street's estimates, though the membership warehouse retailer's per-share profit topped expectations.

Costco shares (COST) slipped around 1% after hours on Thursday.

Sales for the company's fiscal fourth quarter rose 8% to $84.4 billion, with same-store sales up 5.7%. Costco earned $5.87 a share. E-commerce sales rose 13.6% during the quarter.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected Costco to report fourth-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $5.80, on revenue of $86.01 billion and a 5.9% same-store sales increase.

Costco's third-quarter same-store sales also missed Wall Street's estimates.

However, Bryan Hayes, a strategist at Zacks, said the retailer's bottom-line figures beat estimates for ninth time over the past 10 quarters.

"The consistent trend speaks to management's ability to execute in a relatively difficult retail environment," Hayes said over email. "With inflationary pressures lingering throughout the quarter, Americans continued to flock to the membership-based retail chain."

Ahead of the results, analysts cited Costco's size and its ability to find new ways to drive sales as advantages, especially with customers looking for more ways to save money. However, the company has faced concerns over slower growth this year.

In May, Costco executives said they would try to reroute shipments, rely more on local sourcing and use its Kirkland brand in an effort to keep prices low.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: JAPAN SEPT TOKYO CPI: HOTELS FEES +5.8% (+0.08 POINT) VS. +5.3% (+0.08 PT) IN AUG; INBOUND SPENDING SLOWDOWN FADES AS YEN STABLE AFTER FIRMING EARLIER
Banxico Cuts Interest Rate to 7.5%, as Expected
data.description:
The Bank of Mexico cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 bps to 7.5% in its September meeting, in line with market expectations, bringing it to its lowest level since May 2022, and indicated it would consider further easing at future meetings.

The decision came amid ongoing concerns about global trade tensions and sluggish economic growth in the country.

In a statement on Thursday, the bank said it took into account "weak economic growth" and fluctuating global trade policies in its decision to lower borrowing costs.

Wednesday’s data showed annual core inflation hit 4.26% in the first half of September.

Banxico targets inflation at 3%, plus or minus a percentage point.

Headline inflation also accelerated in the first half of September, rising to 3.74% from 3.49% in the first half of August. In updated inflation forecasts released on Thursday, Banxico raised its estimate for year-end annual core inflation to 4.0% in the fourth quarter, up from its previous estimate of 3.7%.

data.title: Hanwha Aerospace Acquires Over 1 Million Shares of NextDecade Corp
data.description:
Reporter NameHanwha Aerospace Co., Ltd.
Relationship10% Owner
TypePurchase
Amount$7,010,032
SEC FilingForm 4

Hanwha Aerospace Co., Ltd., a 10% owner of NextDecade Corp, reported purchasing 1,001,329 shares of Common Stock on September 23 and 24, 2025, for a total amount of $7,010,032. The transactions were made at weighted average prices of $6.9856 and $7.0193, respectively. Following these transactions, Hanwha Aerospace directly owns 18,537,697 shares and indirectly owns 17,536,369 shares through Hanwha Ocean LLC. The report indicates Hanwha Aerospace's disclaim of beneficial ownership except to the extent of its pecuniary interest.

SEC Filing: NextDecade Corp [ NEXT ] - Form 4 - Sep. 25, 2025

data.title: South Korea's Kospi Opens 0.9% Lower at 3440.39
Wellgistics Health Announces Pricing of $5 Million Public Offering
data.description:
TAMPA, FL / ACCESS Newswire / September 25, 2025 / Wellgistics Health, Inc. , a leader in next-generation pharmaceutical distribution, digital prescription routing, and AI-powered hub fulfillment, today announced the pricing of a public offering of 7,142,862 shares of common stock, together with warrants to purchase up to 7,142,862 shares of common stock at a combined public offering price of $0.70 per share and associated warrant. The warrants will have an exercise price of $0.70 per share, will be exercisable upon issuance and will expire five years thereafter. The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about September 29, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

H.C. Wainwright & Co. is acting as the exclusive placement agent for the offering.

The gross proceeds to the Company from the offering are expected to be approximately $5 million, before deducting the placement agent's fees and other offering expenses payable by the Company. The Company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and general corporate purposes, which may include operating expenses, research and development, and pending and future acquisitions.

A registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333-288932) relating to the offering was declared effective by the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") on September 25, 2025. The offering is being made only by means of a prospectus forming part of the effective registration statement relating to the offering. A preliminary prospectus relating to the offering has been filed with the SEC. Electronic copies of the final prospectus, when available, may be obtained on the SEC's website at http://www.sec.gov and may also be obtained, when available, by contacting H.C. Wainwright & Co., LLC at 430 Park Avenue, 3rd Floor, New York, NY 10022, by phone at (212) 856-5711 or e-mail at placements@hcwco.com.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any of the securities described herein, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any state or other jurisdiction in which such an offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such state or other jurisdiction.

About Wellgistics Health

Wellgistics Health, Inc. delivers medications from manufacturer to patient-faster, smarter, and more affordably. Its integrated platform connects more than 6,500 independent pharmacies and 200+ U.S. manufacturers, offering wholesale distribution, digital prescription routing, and AI-driven hub services such as eligibility, adherence, onboarding, prior authorization, and cash-pay fulfillment. As a PBM-agnostic alternative, Wellgistics Health provides compliant, end-to-end solutions designed to restore access, transparency, and trust in U.S. healthcare.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release may contain forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements include statements concerning plans, objectives, goals, strategies, future events or performance, and underlying assumptions and other statements that are other than statements of historical facts. When Wellgistics Health uses words such as "may," "will," "intend," "should," "believe," "expect," "anticipate," "project," "estimate," or similar expressions that do not relate solely to historical matters, it is making forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements include, without limitation, statements regarding Wellgistics Health's strategy and descriptions of its future operations, prospects, and plans. Forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance and involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from Wellgistics Health's expectations discussed in the forward-looking statements. These statements are subject to uncertainties and risks including, but not limited to, market conditions, the completion of the offering, the satisfaction of customary closing conditions related to the offering, the intended use of net proceeds from the offering and other risks detailed in our reports and statements filed with the SEC. Investors are cautioned not to place undue reliance upon any forward-looking statements in this press release. Additional factors are discussed in Wellgistics Health's filings with the SEC, available at www.sec.gov.

Media & Investor Contact

data.title: Nikkei Stock Average Opens 0.3% Lower at 45634.20, Dragged by Pharmaceutical, Machinery Stocks
Quick Finalization of South Korea's $350B U.S. Investment Looks Less Likely — Market Talk
data.description: A quick finalization of South Korea's $350 billion U.S. investment fund deal in exchange for lowering U.S. tariffs is less likely, says Citigroup analyst Jin-Wook Kim in a note. Seoul and Washington have struggled to work out details of the deal, verbally agreed on in July but yet to be formally signed. Both sides could either reach a partial compromise after a prolonged deadlock or see the U.S. impose an earlier proposed 25% or even higher tariff on Korean goods if they fail to reach a final agreement during the Oct. 31-Nov. 1 APEC Korea Summit, which Trump is reportedly attending, Kim says. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Oil Rises Amid Elevated Geopolitical Tensions — Market Talk
data.description: Oil rises in the early Asian session amid elevated geopolitical tensions. European diplomats have concluded that a recent Russian incursion into Estonia was a deliberate tactic ordered by Russian commanders, ANZ Research analysts say in a research report. They have warned that NATO is ready to respond to any further violations of its airspace with full force, the analysts note. "This ratchets up the tensions in the Russia-Ukraine war and raised the prospect of increased pressure on Russia via additional sanctions on its oil industry," they add. Front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.4% higher at $65.26/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.3% higher at $69.63/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

Chiba Bank Makes Statement Following Media Reports About Merger With Chiba Kogyo
The TV Boss Who Kicked Kimmel Off His Stations and Started a Firestorm — WSJ
data.description:
By Joe Flint

Perry Sook has spent decades doing two things very well: building Nexstar Media Group into a television giant and keeping a low profile while doing it.

That changed last week when he greenlighted the decision to drop the ABC late-night show "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" from more than two dozen stations for remarks the host made about the killing of conservative activist Charlie Kirk. Sinclair Broadcast Group followed suit for dozens more stations.

Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr had earlier that day suggested the agency might come down on broadcasters that didn't take action against Kimmel, saying they have a responsibility to act in the public interest. After Nexstar's and Sinclair's decisions to indefinitely pre-empt the show, ABC parent Disney pulled it off the air for four days.

Sook's business decision fueled a national debate over censorship and exposed the often fraught power dynamics of broadcast television. Many Democrats and some conservatives accused Carr of overreach on regulating free speech in the days after his comments about taking action against broadcasters.

The 67-year-old CEO, referred to by some at the company as "Uncle Perry" for his approachable demeanor and high level of engagement with station staff, has more than Nexstar's existing broadcast licenses at stake.

The company owns and operates ABC affiliates in markets including Nashville, Tenn., New Orleans and Salt Lake City, and is in the process of seeking federal approval for its $6.2 billion acquisition of rival Tegna. Sook framed Nexstar's deal for Tegna as a way for local stations to get a fair shake and better compete against tech companies and larger entertainment conglomerates.

To get the Tegna deal done, Sook needs the FCC to scrap rules preventing any company from owning TV stations reaching more than 39% of the country's homes with televisions, and has lobbied the FCC for the change. Nexstar's proposed acquisition of Tegna's stations would give it reach into 80% of such U.S. households.

Sook declined to comment for this article.

In an April letter to shareholders, Sook said he had made several trips to Washington, D.C., to push for regulatory changes that "level the playing field." He highlighted "the unbridled and ubiquitous reach of Big Tech and Big Media and the limits placed on the reach of local broadcasters."

Carr's FCC is reviewing that limit. Sook said in the April letter that "the prospect of meaningful broadcast ownership reform has never been better."

"No one can logically defend the current rules," he wrote. Months later, he praised the Trump administration's initiatives aimed at offering local broadcasters a chance to expand their reach.

Nexstar has said the decision to indefinitely pre-empt Kimmel is unrelated to its Tegna deal. On Wednesday, Nexstar said it continues to evaluate the show and is speaking with Disney "with a focus on ensuring the program reflects and respects the diverse interests of the communities we serve."

Starting small

Sook grew up in Pennsylvania and West Virginia, and worked as a DJ in high school. He has described his upbringing as blue-collar.

He was the second member of his family to attend college, and in 1980 graduated from Ohio University in Athens, Ohio, where he met his wife, Sandy Sook. The couple have three children.

People close to Sook describe him as politically conservative but not outspoken.

Sook tried his hand at TV reporting at a small West Virginia station early in his career, a role he has said gave him respect for journalists. While receiving an award in 2015 from the Radio Television Digital News Association Foundation honoring the First Amendment, he joked about his skills, saying "on-air talent is not a phrase that should be applied uniformly to everyone appearing on the screen."

He shifted into ad sales and, eventually, station management. In 1996, Sook cobbled together enough money to buy WYOU, a Scranton, Pa., television station where he had once worked .

"He was like a kid at Christmas when he came up to tell me he had the money to buy this station," recalled Preston Padden, a former Fox and ABC executive who has known Sook for decades. Fox Corp. and Wall Street Journal parent News Corp share common ownership.

Sook has stressed his affiliates' roles as trustees of public airwaves and purveyors of quality information. He has said he values local journalism, but sees TV as a sales tool.

"Helping local businesses grow and sell stuff — that's our basic mission," he said in a 2023 interview with the Nexstar station in Columbus, Ohio.

From one station in Pennsylvania, Sook built a publicly traded media empire with more than 13,000 employees. It became the nation's largest operator of TV stations in 2019, with the acquisition of Tribune Media for $7.2 billion including debt.

Nexstar has also sought to expand into other corners of media. It launched the NewsNation cable channel in 2020, bought the political news publication the Hill in 2021 and purchased the CW broadcast network in 2022.

NewsNation hasn't made a meaningful dent against its rivals, averaging 62,000 viewers so far this year, according to Nielsen data. By comparison, Fox News garnered 1.8 million and MSNBC attracted 551,000.

Power struggles

When Sook started out in TV, the relationships between networks and affiliates were fairly straightforward. An affiliate typically gave the majority of its ad time to the network in return for content and financial compensation.

As more TV stations popped up and the competitive landscape changed, networks gained more leverage. Affiliates now pay the networks for content, in addition to giving up most of their ad time for network shows. Affiliates also share revenue they receive from pay-TV distributors that pay to carry their stations.

And local affiliates are no longer the exclusive home of network content. Today, they are just the first stop for network shows before they land on streaming services such as Hulu, Peacock and Paramount+, often within 24 hours of their broadcast airing.

"The past three to four decades have largely been characterized by networks demanding and receiving financial concessions from their affiliate body. Rarely have affiliates possessed the leverage to fight back," said Sandy Grushow, former Fox TV chairman.

While local stations seldom refuse to carry a show, Nexstar has in the past complained to ABC about Kimmel's political commentary, people familiar with the matter said.

The FCC's Carr has framed recent tension between affiliates and ABC as a David vs. Goliath battle. He posted on social media Tuesday that Democrats "simply can't stand that local TV stations — for the first time in years — stood up to a national programmer & chose to exercise their lawful right to pre-empt programming."

Write to Joe Flint at Joe.Flint@wsj.com

data.title: Inside the Yearslong Effort to Rebuild a Florida Island After Hurricane Ian — WSJ
data.description:
By Jessica Flint | Photographs by Thomas Simonetti for WSJ

Tiffany McQuaid was a month away from listing a multimillion-dollar beachfront estate on Sanibel Island in southwest Florida. Then Hurricane Ian made landfall near there on Sept. 28, 2022.

The storm caused catastrophic destruction, particularly on Sanibel and neighboring islands, and in Fort Myers down to Naples, a roughly 30-mile drive south. Portions of the Sanibel Causeway, which connects the island to the mainland, washed away, becoming a symbol of the devastation.

The hurricane left a landscape of debris across the approximately 16-square-mile island, beloved for its Old Florida charm and shell-filled beaches. Many buildings, especially older ones, were ruined. Some surviving structures with water damage were affected by mold. Vibrant green vegetation turned brown from salt exposure.

McQuaid, a real-estate broker with Serhant, said her 11,000-square-foot listing, built in 2009 and designed to exceed Florida's residential building code, largely withstood the storm. There was minor exterior damage, such as 32 cracked roof tiles, and trees down on its 2.25 acres.

But with so much residential and commercial property to rebuild across Sanibel, McQuaid delayed the listing until the island began bouncing back. Recovery overall has been slow.

"Ian has shocked us all with how long it's taken to rebuild," says Jeff Burns, a real-estate agent with Premier Sotheby's International Realty who grew up on the island. "With other hurricanes, within six months, it seemed like it was life as usual."

A year and a half after Ian, the area's recovery was at 28%, according to Sanibel Solutions, a coalition of residents, civic leaders and business owners tracking everything from housing and vacation rentals to business reopenings and community services. Now it's at 65%.

"It's all about perspective," McQuaid says. "If you saw Sanibel post-Ian, you'd marvel at where it is today." McQuaid eventually put the beachfront property on the market in June 2024, and it is currently listed for $33.88 million.

The prolonged recovery in the wake of Ian, coupled with residents' belief in the resilience of the island, has created an unusual housing market that is only partly apparent in the data.

Sanibel's post-Ian real-estate activity initially surged. Undamaged homes built to current code sold at only a slight discount, and long-held properties that might have never traded without the hurricane came on the market. Some sellers, unwilling or financially unable to endure multiyear rebuilds, offered markdowns. Buyers included investors, fix-and-flippers, those previously priced out and current owners moving around the island.

Eric Pfeifer, broker-owner of Pfeifer Realty Group who has lived and worked in real estate on Sanibel since 2001, says that most sellers who left were older or wanted to be closer to family, not necessarily because of fear of another storm.

Ian made landfall near the island with roughly 130 mph winds. Residents say that flooding from the unprecedented storm surge, which exceeded 10 feet in some Sanibel areas, made this hurricane different.

Post-disaster, many residents grappled with home-, flood- and wind-insurance negotiations, leaving some homeowners in limbo for a year or more, unable to start rebuilding their properties until claims were settled. Condominium owners navigated complex approvals involving many stakeholders. A contractor and material shortage drove up prices. There were still a large number of workers, which increased traffic and slowed commutes to job sites.

Today, three years after Ian, recovery is visible. About two-thirds of Sanibel's pre-Ian restaurants, including the Lighthouse Cafe and Doc Ford's Rum Bar & Grille, have reopened, according to Sanibel Solutions. More than 80% of the over 500 businesses on the island are back, and two-thirds of the roughly 1,900 units in the short-term rental district are operational. The foliage is turning vibrant green.

Sanibel has been affected by hurricanes since Ian, and residents know there will be more. "This is why the Sanibel community is making a diligent effort to build back more resiliently," Pfeifer says.

Childhood friends Justin O'Brien, 40, and Tim Goff, 41, invested in building more storm-resistant homes after the hurricane because they said the island's lifestyle and beaches will always lure residents. Following Ian, they bought roughly 10 houses on Sanibel and flipped all but one, averaging a 10% to 12% profit per property.

"We wanted to be part of the recovery and invest in the community," O'Brien says. He and Goff are Fort Myers natives and co-owners of the southwest Florida restaurant chain 3 Pepper Burrito Co.

O'Brien and his wife, Carley O'Brien, 28, moved into the one home they didn't flip — a $700,000 house that had been devastated in the hurricane. The home is 1,700 square feet with three bedrooms on 0.5 acres. They invested approximately $450,000 in renovations, giving priority to storm durability. They replaced roofing, plumbing, electrical, air conditioning, kitchen appliances and landscaping.

Some Sanibel homes, especially older ones that didn't meet current flood-elevation code, have been raised onto concrete pilings. But the friends ultimately decided against what they estimated would be a $200,000 expense. Hurricanes hitting again are definitely a possibility, O'Brien says. "A flood of that level? That was a risk we were willing to take."

Despite the rebuilding progress, the housing market is in flux. Sanibel had an average of 465 active listings a month from January to July this year, according to Redfin data. That's up 67% from the average number of listings in the comparable seven-month periods each year from 2016 to 2019. In contrast, sales are down 31% from those same pre-Covid years. This year through July, 163 homes have sold.

However, isolating the storm's impact on the market is challenging. Only months before Ian hit, Sanibel's median home-sale price reached $1.4 million, according to Redfin data, driven by skyrocketing demand along much of coastal Florida during the Covid-19 pandemic. Interest rates in the U.S. began climbing before the storm. "It's not 100% accurate to attribute the current market all to the storm," says real-estate agent Tiffany Burns, business partner and wife of Jeff Burns. The couple has worked in real estate on Sanibel for more than 20 years.

Homeowners' insurance is relatively straightforward, usually calculated as a square-footage multiple. But wind-insurance affordability depends on factors such as age and construction, and flood insurance costs vary significantly. Pfeifer says that pre-Ian, many cash buyers self-insured, which most buyers are less likely to do now.

Still, comparing sales prices on Sanibel from 2019 to 2025 suggests that most homes have appreciated 3% to 4% a year, Pfeifer says. The median sale price was $848,000 in July, according to Redfin.

Sanibel-based interior designer Diane Knight, who has worked on the island for more than two decades, has noticed people aren't just rebuilding what was lost. "Homes are really being upgraded," she says. Her family-run business's approach to designing colorful, comfortable and casual spaces hasn't changed since Ian, but she says clients are now undertaking architectural enhancements they previously weren't considering, including adding custom cabinetry, crown molding and coffered ceilings.

"So many of the condos and homes were built in the late 1970s as simple beach getaway homes and did not have architectural details," Knight says, noting that starting over from scratch has given owners the opportunity to bring properties up-to-date.

That is what Mark Bogosian, 38, has done on Captiva, which is connected to Sanibel by a short bridge. He bought a $2 million house on Captiva that needed repairs after Ian and that he now uses primarily as a short-term rental. Bogosian, the founder of Longfellow Design Build, which is based in New England and Florida, spent about $1.5 million to fully renovate the roughly 5,000-square-foot, five-bedroom home. He added high-end finishes, a swimming pool and a guesthouse.

"The silver lining through all of this is we are coming back stronger and more beautiful than before," says lifelong Sanibel resident Phaidra McDermott, broker-owner of the island's Kingfisher Real Estate. She has worked in real estate for 30 years. "We anticipate a major shift in the market once the new, more resilient inventory is available."

Write to Jessica Flint at Jessica.Flint@wsj.com

data.title: Majestic's HKEX-Listed Subsidiary Persistence Announces HK$472 Million Financing
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, British Columbia –September 25, 2025 – TheNewswire - Majestic GoldCorp. (“Majestic” or the “Company”)(TSX.V: MJS, FSE: A0BK1D) ) is pleased toannounce that its 70.5% owned subsidiary, Persistence Resources GroupLtd. (“Persistence”) (Stock Code: 2489), has entered into aprivate placement financing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange(“HKEX”) for gross proceeds of up to HK$472 million (approximatelyCAD$84.5 million) (“Placement”).

Under the terms of the Placement, Persistence willissue up to 400 million common shares (the “Shares”) at a price ofHK$1.18 per Share (approximately CAD$0.211 per Share) on the HKEX. The Share price of HK$1.18 represents a discount of
approximately 19.2% to the closing price of HK$1.46 per Share
(approximately CAD$0.261 per Share) as quoted on the HKEX on the date
of the placing agreement; and a discount of approximately 18.5% to the
average closing price of approximately HK$1.448 per Share
(approximately CAD$0.259 per Share) as quoted on the HKEX for the last
five consecutive trading days prior to the date of the placing
agreement. Persistence will pay, if any, up to 1.5% in placing
agents’ commissions.


The Shares represent approximately 20.0% of the total
issued and outstanding shares of Persistence as of the date of this
announcement and approximately 16.7% of the enlarged total number of
issued shares upon completion of the Placement.


Persistence intends to use the estimated net proceeds
of the Placement primarily for (i) potential gold mine project
acquisitions; (ii) acceleration of its business expansion; and (iii)
general working capital and other general corporate purposes.


Upon successful completion of the Placement,
Majestic’s ownership interest in Persistence will be reduced from
70.5% to approximately 58.8%.


Closing of the Placement is subject to obtaining all
required approvals, including all regulatory approvals.


About Majestic Gold


Majestic Gold Corp., a
British Columbia based company, is a low-cost junior gold producer
engaged in commercial gold production at the Songjiagou Gold Mine in
eastern Shandong Province, China. Additional information on the
Company and its projects is available at www.sedarplus.ca and on theCompany’s website at www.majesticgold.com.

For further information, pleasecontact:

James Mackie, CFO and CorporateSecretary

Telephone: (604) 560-9060

Email: info@majesticgold.com

Website: www.majesticgold.com

Cautionary Notes

Certain statements contained hereinmay constitute forward‐looking statements and are made pursuant tothe "safe harbor" provisions of the United States PrivateSecurities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 and Canadian securities laws.Forward‐looking statements are statements which relate to futureevents. Such statements include estimates, forecasts, and statements as to management'sexpectations with respect to, among other things, business andfinancial prospects, financial multiples and accretion estimates,future trends, plans, strategies, objectives, and expectations,including with respect to production, exploration drilling, reservesand resources, exploitation activities and events or futureoperations. Information inferred from the interpretation of drillingresults and information concerning mineral resource estimates may alsobe deemed to be forward-looking statements, as it constitutes aprediction of what might be found to be present when, and if, aproject is actually developed.

In some cases, you can identifyforward‐looking statements by terminology such as "may","should", "expects", "plans,"anticipates", believes", "estimates","predicts", "potential", or "continue"or the negative of these terms or other comparable terminology. Thesestatements are only predictions and involve known and unknown risks,uncertainties and other factors that may cause our or ourindustry's actual results, level of activity, performance, orachievements to be materially different from any future results,levels of activity, performance, or achievements expressed or impliedby these forward‐looking statements.

While these forward‐lookingstatements, and any assumptions upon which they are based, are made ingood faith, and reflect our current judgment regarding the directionof our business, actual results will almost always vary, sometimesmaterially, from any estimates, predictions, projections, assumptions,or other future performance suggestions herein. data.title: Tokyo Core Inflation Below Forecasts in Sept
data.description:
Core consumer prices in Tokyo’s Ku-area rose 2.5% year-on-year in September 2025, matching August’s pace and falling short of expectations for an uptick to 2.8%.

Despite the miss, inflation stayed above the Bank of Japan’s 2% target, fueling speculation of another rate hike later this year.

Minutes from the BOJ’s July meeting showed policymakers were prepared to keep tightening if the economy and prices evolve as projected.

At its September meeting, the BOJ left rates unchanged but faced two dissents, signaling further hikes may be imminent.

Officials have largely paused the cycle amid concerns over the impact of US tariffs on Japan’s export-reliant economy.

data.title: US probes Waaree for solar tariff evasion, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The U.S. Customs and Border Protection is investigating whether Waaree Energies and Waaree Solar Americas evaded anti-dumping and countervailing duties on solar cells from China and other Southeast Asian nations, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

South Korea passes landmark bill to legalise tattoo artists
data.description:
South Korea's parliament has passed a bill to legalise tattoo artists, ending a decades-long ban on tattooing by those without medical licences.

The Tattoist Act passed convincingly on Thursday afternoon with 195 votes in favour out of 202 members present, enabling non-medical professionals to obtain a licence. The new law will take effect after a two-year grace period.

South Korea is the only developed country where tattooing is considered a procedure that only medical professionals are capable of legally performing.

While tattoos are not illegal, tattoo artists face fines of up to 50 million won ($35,464) as well as prison terms for practising their craft.

Despite the ban, Korean tattoos have become increasingly popular at home and abroad, making it easy to spot people on the streets of Korean cities with tattoos including K-pop stars.

Tattoo artists welcomed the act's passage after years fighting to end the ban.

"Tattooists in Korea, each working in their own spaces, overcame the isolating nature of our profession and built a solidarity of 1,100 members," said Doy, a famous South Korean famous tattoo artist and union organiser who has been prosecuted and fined for practising his profession.

"In doing so, we ourselves changed the injustice that had long denied our profession."

President Lee Jae Myung, who has supported legalising tattoists, is expected to sign it into a law.

($1 = 1,409.8700 won)

data.title: China Signals U.S. Soybean Purchases Contingent on Tariff Removal
data.description:
China would purchase U.S. soybeans if the United States removes what it calls “unreasonable tariffs” and creates conditions to support bilateral trade, a Chinese commerce ministry spokesperson said Thursday.

Despite being the world’s largest soybean buyer, China has yet to book U.S. cargoes from the autumn harvest, favoring South American supplies instead.

Senior Chinese trade negotiator Li Chenggang recently met U.S. Midwest political and business leaders, signaling potential purchases ahead of broader trade talks.

However, disagreements over technical details are complicating negotiations, with Chinese and U.S. officials set to meet again at the U.S. Treasury later today.
